name: Intel Antes Up $1B on Success of Itanium Line (USA Today)
description: Michelle Kessler's USA Today story (1 March 2002) on Intel's Itanium line and the McKinley successor preview. Reports $1 billion six-year HP+Intel Itanium investment, $1000-$4000 chip pricing, and the chicken-and-egg software adoption problem caused by Itanium's 64-bit architecture incompatibility with 32-bit Intel. Quotes Aberdeen Group's Peter Kastner: 'Intel and H-P are holding a party, and nobody's coming.' Notes Intel's reported backup plan to develop a 64-bit version of its 32-bit processor line (foreshadowing AMD64/x86-64 response and Intel's eventual EM64T). Cites HP+Compaq merger as additional Itanium-volume risk.
